title: plectranthus concinnus
intro: Plectranthus concinnus, also known as Andean mint or huacapurana, is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Plectranthus concinnus include relief from digestive issues, headaches, and fever, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and essential oils.
